questions.question_text: Which of the following is an autotrophic organism?
questions.explanation: Green plants are autotrophs because they make their own food by photosynthesis. Animals and fungi cannot prepare their own food in this way.

options.option_text: Grass
Goat
Mushroom
Earthworm

questions.question_text: During a starch test on a leaf, which observation shows that starch is present?
questions.explanation: When iodine solution is added to a leaf containing starch, the leaf turns blue-black. This indicates that photosynthesis has produced food stored as starch.

options.option_text: The leaf turns bright red
The leaf turns blue-black
The leaf becomes transparent
The leaf gives out bubbles

questions.question_text: Plants mainly take in carbon dioxide from the air through tiny openings on their leaves called:
questions.explanation: The tiny pores on the leaf surface are called stomata. They help in exchange of gases such as carbon dioxide and oxygen.

options.option_text: Roots
Veins
Stomata
Petals

questions.question_text: Green plants need sunlight to make food.
questions.explanation: Sunlight provides the energy needed for photosynthesis. Without it, most green plants cannot prepare food normally.

options.option_text: True
False

questions.question_text: Which plant part usually absorbs water and minerals from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals from the soil. The absorbed materials are then transported to other parts of the plant.

options.option_text: Flower
Fruit
Root
Bud

questions.question_text: The green pigment that helps plants trap sunlight is called:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment present mainly in leaves. It absorbs light energy needed for photosynthesis.

options.option_text: Hemoglobin
Chlorophyll
Plasma
Pollen

questions.question_text: Which of these is a non-green plant that cannot make its own food?
questions.explanation: A mushroom does not contain chlorophyll and cannot perform photosynthesis. It depends on other sources for food.

options.option_text: Spinach
Neem
Mushroom
Grass

questions.question_text: Which of the following is known as a parasite in plant nutrition?
questions.explanation: Cuscuta depends on another plant for food and support, so it is a parasitic plant.

options.option_text: Mango
Rose
Cactus
Cuscuta

questions.question_text: Plants store the food they prepare mainly in the form of:
questions.explanation: Much of the food made during photosynthesis is stored as starch in different plant parts.

options.option_text: Protein only
Water
Starch
Oxygen

questions.question_text: Roots of all plants remain above the ground.
questions.explanation: This statement is false. In most plants, roots grow below the ground and help in absorption and anchorage.

options.option_text: True
False

questions.question_text: A student keeps one potted plant in sunlight and another in a dark cupboard for two days. Which result is most likely?
questions.explanation: The plant kept in sunlight can continue photosynthesis and remain healthier. The plant in darkness cannot make enough food and may begin to look weak.

options.option_text: The plant in sunlight stays healthier because it can make food
The plant in darkness makes more food because it stays cool
Both plants make the same amount of food
The dark-kept plant grows faster because it avoids sunlight

questions.question_text: Riya wants to show that carbon dioxide is needed for photosynthesis. What should she do next after taking two similar potted plants?
questions.explanation: To compare fairly, one plant should get normal air and the other should have carbon dioxide removed around its leaves. Then the leaves can be tested for starch.

options.option_text: Put both plants in complete darkness
Keep one plant where carbon dioxide is available and remove carbon dioxide around the other
Remove all leaves from both plants
Water one plant with colored water only

questions.question_text: After boiling a leaf in alcohol and then adding iodine solution, only the green part turns blue-black. What does this show?
questions.explanation: Only the green part has chlorophyll, so only that part could make starch by photosynthesis. The blue-black color confirms starch presence there.

options.option_text: Non-green parts absorb more water
Only green parts prepared starch
Iodine colors every leaf part equally
Alcohol creates starch in the leaf

questions.question_text: Small drops of water are seen on the inside of a transparent plastic bag tied around a leafy branch. What can be inferred?
questions.explanation: The leaves lose water vapor to the air. This process is called transpiration, and the water later condenses on the bag.

options.option_text: Leaves absorb water from the plastic bag
Roots are leaking water into the air
Leaves are losing water vapor by transpiration
The bag is making new water by itself

questions.question_text: Why are many farmers advised to water plants in the early morning or evening rather than at noon?
questions.explanation: At noon the temperature is usually high, so more water is lost quickly by evaporation and transpiration. Morning or evening watering reduces this loss.

options.option_text: Plants cannot absorb water after sunrise
Less water is lost quickly at cooler times of the day
Sunlight destroys water in the soil completely
Roots grow only in the evening

questions.question_text: Which statement correctly explains why mushrooms are not placed with green plants in food-making ability?
questions.explanation: Mushrooms do not have chlorophyll and cannot prepare food by photosynthesis. Therefore, they depend on other sources for nutrition.

options.option_text: Mushrooms have more roots than green plants
Mushrooms absorb sunlight faster than leaves
Mushrooms do not contain chlorophyll, so they cannot photosynthesize
Mushrooms prepare food only at night

questions.question_text: Which pair is correctly matched with its type of nutrition?
questions.explanation: Cuscuta is parasitic because it takes food from another plant. Mushroom is saprotrophic because it feeds on dead and decaying matter.

options.option_text: Cuscuta - autotrophic; mushroom - parasitic
Cuscuta - parasitic; mushroom - saprotrophic
Rose - saprotrophic; algae - parasitic
Grass - parasitic; neem - saprotrophic

questions.question_text: Why are leaves called the food factories of the plant?
questions.explanation: Leaves are called food factories because they contain chlorophyll and usually make food by photosynthesis using sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water.

accepted_answers: Leaves make food for the plant by photosynthesis.
Leaves contain chlorophyll and prepare food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide.

questions.question_text: A potted plant kept near a window slowly bends toward the light. This response is mainly related to:
questions.explanation: Plants often grow toward light because they need light for photosynthesis. This directional growth response helps them receive more sunlight.

options.option_text: Seed dispersal
Growth response to light
Formation of pollen
Water storage in roots

questions.question_text: Why are broad green leaves better food-making structures than dry brown leaves?
questions.explanation: Broad green leaves usually have more chlorophyll and a larger surface area to trap sunlight and exchange gases, so they are better suited for photosynthesis.

options.option_text: Dry brown leaves absorb more carbon dioxide
Broad leaves cannot lose water
Green broad leaves can trap more light and contain chlorophyll
Brown leaves have more stomata than green leaves

questions.question_text: A student says, "Plants only respire at night." Which correction is correct?
questions.explanation: Plants respire all the time, day and night. Photosynthesis usually happens in sunlight, but respiration continues continuously.

options.option_text: Plants respire only in winter
Plants respire both day and night
Plants do not respire at all
Plants respire only when watered

questions.question_text: Which comparison between roots and stomata is correct?
questions.explanation: Roots mainly absorb water and minerals from the soil, while stomata help in exchange of gases and also in loss of water vapor.

options.option_text: Roots exchange gases; stomata absorb minerals
Roots make chlorophyll; stomata form seeds
Roots absorb water and minerals; stomata help in gas exchange
Roots release pollen; stomata anchor the plant

questions.question_text: Why is a leaf boiled in alcohol during the starch test experiment?
questions.explanation: Alcohol removes chlorophyll from the leaf. This makes the leaf colorless so the blue-black iodine result can be seen clearly.

options.option_text: To add more starch to the leaf
To make the leaf absorb more sunlight
To remove chlorophyll so the iodine result can be seen clearly
To stop water from entering the leaf forever

questions.question_text: Name any two raw materials needed by a plant to prepare food.
questions.explanation: Plants use water and carbon dioxide as raw materials. Sunlight provides energy, and chlorophyll helps trap that light.

questions.question_text: A child wants to know whether roots absorb colored water. What is the best next step?
questions.explanation: Placing a plant in colored water and observing whether the color appears in the stem or veins is a good way to investigate upward movement of water.

options.option_text: Put a plant or cut stem in colored water and watch for color movement
Keep the plant in a dark room without water
Cover the roots with oil
Remove all the leaves first

questions.question_text: Two similar plants are kept under glass jars. Jar A contains normal air. Jar B contains a substance that absorbs carbon dioxide. After sunlight exposure, the leaf from plant B shows no blue-black color with iodine. What is the best conclusion?
questions.explanation: The only major difference was the presence of carbon dioxide. Since the leaf in Jar B did not form starch, carbon dioxide is necessary for photosynthesis.

options.option_text: Sunlight is not needed for photosynthesis
Water alone makes starch in leaves
Carbon dioxide is necessary for photosynthesis
Iodine prevents starch formation

questions.question_text: Why should a leaf not be heated directly over a flame while removing chlorophyll with alcohol during a school experiment?
questions.explanation: Alcohol catches fire easily, so it should be heated indirectly using a water bath. This makes the experiment safer.

options.option_text: Because alcohol freezes near a flame
Because chlorophyll escapes too quickly in light
Because alcohol is inflammable and can catch fire
Because iodine stops working near a flame

questions.question_text: A gardener notices that leaves of a healthy potted plant are coated with a thick layer of dust for many days. Which effect is most likely if the dust is not cleaned?
questions.explanation: Dust can block some sunlight and may also affect stomata on leaf surfaces. This can reduce photosynthesis and weaken the plant over time.

options.option_text: The plant will immediately stop absorbing water through roots
The plant will make more food because dust adds minerals
Photosynthesis may reduce because light and gas exchange are affected
The plant will become parasitic

questions.question_text: Select all statements that are correct about stomata.
questions.explanation: Stomata are tiny pores mostly on leaves. They help in exchange of gases and also allow water vapor to escape during transpiration.

options.option_text: They help carbon dioxide enter the leaf
They allow water vapor to escape
They absorb minerals from the soil
They produce seeds

questions.question_text: A wilted plant becomes fresh again a few hours after watering. Which sequence best explains this?
questions.explanation: After watering, roots absorb water, transport it upward, and cells regain firmness. This makes the leaves and stem stand upright again.

options.option_text: Leaves directly absorb soil and become heavier
Roots absorb water, water moves upward, and plant cells regain firmness
Flowers produce chlorophyll and straighten the stem
Stomata pull minerals into the roots

questions.question_text: Which piece of evidence best supports the idea that chlorophyll is important for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: In a variegated leaf, only the green regions contain chlorophyll. If only those parts give a starch test, that is strong evidence for chlorophyll's role.

options.option_text: A dry leaf falls from a plant in summer
A root grows deeper in the soil
Only green parts of a variegated leaf test positive for starch
A flower opens in the morning

questions.question_text:
Assertion: Plants kept in total darkness for many days may become weak.
Reason: Without light, they cannot make enough food by photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Both the assertion and reason are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Light is needed for photosynthesis.

options.option_text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason does not explain Assertion
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true

questions.question_text: How does transpiration help a plant even though it causes water loss?
questions.explanation: Transpiration helps pull water upward from the roots, cools the plant, and supports movement of minerals through the plant.

accepted_answers: It helps in upward movement of water and minerals and can cool the plant.
It creates a pull for water movement and also helps cool the plant.

questions.question_text: A student covers part of a green leaf with black paper and keeps the plant in sunlight. After a starch test, only the uncovered part turns blue-black. What can be concluded?
questions.explanation: The covered portion did not receive sunlight, so it could not photosynthesize and form starch. Sunlight is therefore necessary for starch formation in the leaf.

options.option_text: Covered parts produce more oxygen
Black paper adds starch to the leaf
Sunlight is necessary for photosynthesis in the leaf
Iodine reacts only with black paper

questions.question_text: Why do leaves of some indoor plants become pale and weak when kept for a long time away from light?
questions.explanation: Without enough light, photosynthesis decreases. As a result, the plant cannot make enough food and may lose healthy green growth.

options.option_text: Because roots stop anchoring the plant indoors
Because reduced light lowers photosynthesis and food production
Because leaves turn into saprotrophs indoors
Because indoor air has no oxygen

questions.question_text: Which is the best explanation for why cactus leaves are modified into spines?
questions.explanation: Cacti live in dry areas. Spines reduce water loss, while the stem becomes green and carries out photosynthesis.

options.option_text: Spines absorb more carbon dioxide than leaves
Spines make starch directly from soil
Spines help reduce water loss in dry conditions
Spines produce flowers faster

questions.question_text: In an experiment, one plant is watered regularly and another is not watered for several days. Both are kept in sunlight. Which result most directly shows the role of water in photosynthesis and plant health?
questions.explanation: The water-starved plant will wilt and its food-making process will be affected because water is both a raw material and necessary for plant functioning.

options.option_text: The watered plant loses all chlorophyll
The unwatered plant makes more starch
The unwatered plant wilts and cannot function normally
Both plants remain exactly the same

questions.question_text: Select all conditions that are important for normal photosynthesis in a green plant.
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis needs chlorophyll, sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide. Soil itself is not a direct raw material in the photosynthesis equation.

options.option_text: Sunlight
Carbon dioxide
Water
Dry dust on leaves

questions.question_text: A climber plant grows in a shaded corner but stretches toward a brighter region. What is the most reasonable explanation?
questions.explanation: Plants often grow toward light because light is necessary for photosynthesis. Reaching brighter areas helps them make more food.

options.option_text: The plant is trying to escape the soil
The stem wants to become a root
The plant is responding to light so it can photosynthesize better
The plant is searching for insects to eat

questions.question_text: Why is photosynthesis important not only for plants but also for animals?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis makes food at the start of many food chains and releases oxygen into the atmosphere. Animals depend directly or indirectly on this process.

accepted_answers: It provides food directly or indirectly and also releases oxygen.
Animals depend on food made by plants, and photosynthesis also adds oxygen to the air.

questions.question_text: A school gardener can only improve one condition for a weak green plant today: more light, a painted pot, colored ribbons, or a new name tag. Which choice is most scientifically useful first?
questions.explanation: More light directly supports photosynthesis. The other choices do not improve the plant's food-making process.

options.option_text: Move it where it gets better light
Paint the pot blue
Tie colored ribbons to the stem
Add a new name tag

questions.question_text: Which observation best shows that water can travel upward through a stem?
questions.explanation: If a white flower or cut stem placed in colored water shows color in upper parts later, it indicates upward movement of water.

options.option_text: A root becomes muddy after rain
Colored water later appears in upper veins or petals
A seed sinks in water
A dry leaf falls from a plant

questions.question_text: If many stomata on a leaf become blocked, which chain of effects is most likely?
questions.explanation: Blocked stomata reduce gas exchange and also disturb transpiration. This can lower photosynthesis and affect the plant's health.

options.option_text: More seeds form immediately and the plant grows faster
Roots stop existing
Gas exchange and transpiration reduce, which can lower photosynthesis
The leaf becomes a flower

questions.question_text: Which is the best explanation for why some insectivorous plants still perform photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: These plants are green and make food by photosynthesis. They trap insects mainly to obtain certain nutrients, especially where the soil is poor.

options.option_text: They trap insects because they cannot make food at all
They photosynthesize but trap insects to obtain extra nutrients
They trap insects only to get sunlight
They are parasites on all nearby plants

questions.question_text:
Assertion: A leaf with more chlorophyll usually appears greener.
Reason: Chlorophyll is the pigment that gives leaves their green color.
questions.explanation: Both statements are true, and the reason correctly explains the assertion. Chlorophyll is responsible for the green appearance of leaves.

options.option_text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason does not explain Assertion
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true

questions.question_text: State one difference between saprotrophic nutrition and parasitic nutrition.
questions.explanation: Saprotrophs obtain food from dead and decaying matter, while parasites obtain food from a living host.

accepted_answers: Saprotrophs feed on dead and decaying matter, while parasites feed on a living host.
In saprotrophic nutrition food comes from dead material, but in parasitic nutrition it comes from a living organism.

questions.question_text: If a plant has green stems but very tiny leaves, what can still be concluded in many such plants?
questions.explanation: In some plants such as cacti, the stem is green and performs photosynthesis even when leaves are reduced or modified.

options.option_text: The plant cannot make food at all
The roots must be absent
Photosynthesis may occur in the green stem
The plant is always parasitic

questions.question_text: Select all correct statements about roots.
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant and absorb water and minerals. In some plants, roots can also store food.

options.option_text: They help anchor the plant in the soil
They absorb water and minerals
They trap sunlight directly better than leaves
Some roots can store food

questions.question_text: Why might leaves droop more quickly on a hot, windy day than on a cool, calm day if water supply is low?
questions.explanation: Hot and windy conditions increase water loss by transpiration. If roots cannot replace that water fast enough, the leaves droop sooner.

options.option_text: Because wind adds starch to leaves
Because cool air always removes chlorophyll
Because transpiration becomes faster and water loss increases
Because sunlight turns roots into stems

questions.question_text: A student wants to grow a healthy bean plant for a class project. Which plan is the most sensible?
questions.explanation: A healthy bean plant needs light, water, air, and suitable soil conditions. The most sensible choice is the one that supports all basic needs together.

options.option_text: Keep it in a closed box and water it once a month
Give it only colored light and no water
Keep it in sunlight, water it regularly, and allow air around it
Remove most leaves to help it save food forever
